Video.summary: What makes ancient Egypt so interesting? How do we know what we know about it?
Video.title: Introduction to the History of Ancient Egypt

Image.alt: Introduction to the History of Ancient Egypt
Introduction to the History of Ancient Egypt
Introduction to the History of Ancient Egypt

Video.summary: In this lesson, examine the difficulties of studying a prehistoric civilization.
Video.title: Prehistoric Egypt

Video.summary: What role did mythology, religion, and philosophy play in the lives of ancient Egyptians?
Video.title: Ancient Egyptian Thought

Video.summary: Why does modern Egyptology begin with Napoleon?
Video.title: Napoleon and the Beginnings of Egyptology

Image.alt: Napoleon and the Beginnings of Egyptology
Napoleon and the Beginnings of Egyptology
Napoleon and the Beginnings of Egyptology

Video.summary: Learn the four scripts of ancient Egyptian, and the three ways to use hieroglyphic signs.
Video.title: The Rosetta Stone, and Much More

Image.alt: The Rosetta Stone, and Much More
The Rosetta Stone, and Much More
The Rosetta Stone, and Much More

Video.summary: Learn how Egypt, under King Narmer, become history\'s first nation.
Video.title: The First Nation in History

Video.summary: As Egypt becomes a great nation led by a single ruler, traditions arise that will last for millennia.
Video.title: The Rise of the Old Kingdom

Image.alt: The Rise of the Old Kingdom
The Rise of the Old Kingdom
The Rise of the Old Kingdom

Video.summary: This lesson presents a portrait of the founder of the "Fabulous Fourth" Dynasty, Sneferu.
Video.title: Sneferu, the Pyramid Builder

Video.summary: Although there is much to dislike about industrialization, we should nevertheless be grateful for the Industrial Revolution. Investigate why in this opening episode.
Video.title: Industrialization Is Good for You

Video.summary: Start at the beginning in the British Isles, where relative political stability and entrepreneurs willing to experiment with innovation contributed to the birth of the Industrial Revolution.
Video.title: Why Was Britain First?

Video.summary: In Britain in the 18th century, new agricultural methods came into being, freeing up thousands of workers to move into manufacturing work. Take a look at some of these changes to agriculture.
Video.title: The Agricultural Revolution

Video.summary: Traverse the country to see where industry took off, starting with a detailed look at the advantages and dangers of life in London. Then, shift your attention to provincial cities and towns.
Video.title: Cities and Manufacturing Traditions

Video.summary: Explore the world of 18th-century shipyards, where the organization of work, materials, logistics, and complex construction would provide a blueprint for later factory-era industrialization.
Video.title: The Royal Shipyards

Video.summary: Learn about key inventors' innovations, including the flying shuttle, the spinning jenny, and the cotton gin.
Video.title: The Textile Industry

Video.summary: Dive into one of the most dangerous jobs in the world, see what problems miners had to overcome.
Video.title: Coal Mining

Video.summary: After reviewing the history of iron, study how to produce pig iron and forge wrought iron.
Video.title: Iron - Coking and Puddling

Video.summary: We are finally able to reveal the fascinating truths about ancient North America.
Video.title: The Unknown Story of Ancient North America

Image.alt: The Unknown Story of Ancient North America
The Unknown Story of Ancient North America
The Unknown Story of Ancient North America

Video.summary: DNA evidence points to Asia as the origin of all human migration to North America.
Video.title: The First Human Migrations to the Americas

Image.alt: The First Human Migrations to the Americas
The First Human Migrations to the Americas
The First Human Migrations to the Americas

Video.summary: Explore Clovis, the very first American culture.
Video.title: Clovis Man: America's First Culture

Video.summary: Better understand why some Paleo-Indian groups developed hunting and gathering culture.
Video.title: The Archaic Period: Diversity Begins

Video.summary: See how, peoples of North America developed corn agriculture, permanent houses and metallurgy.
Video.title: Late Archaic Innovations

Video.summary: While most North Americans were nomadic, see how a group of ancient people developed a planned community.
Video.title: Poverty Point: North America's First City

Image.alt: Poverty Point: North America's First City
Poverty Point: North America's First City
Poverty Point: North America's First City

Video.summary: Dr. Barnhart takes you on a whirlwind tour of important places, civilizations, and events in Mesoamerica.
Video.grandparentTitle: Maya to Aztec: Ancient Mesoamerica Revealed
Video.title: The Maya, Aztecs, and Mesoamerica

Video.summary: Here, hear how the Olmec were discovered, and investigate three intriguing sites where they lived.
Video.grandparentTitle: Maya to Aztec: Ancient Mesoamerica Revealed
Video.title: Olmec Civilization Emerges
